title: Multiple Granularity Lock Acquisition Algorithm
description: Algorithm showing how locks are acquired at different granularity levels following MGL protocol
code:
PROCEDURE AcquireLock(transaction, node, lockType)
BEGIN
 // Check if parent lock exists for fine-grained locking
 IF node.parent != NULL THEN
 parentLock = GetLock(transaction, node.parent)
 IF lockType == S OR lockType == X THEN
 IF parentLock == NULL THEN
 RETURN DENY // Must have intent lock on parent
 END IF
 IF lockType == X AND parentLock != IX AND parentLock != SIX THEN
 RETURN DENY // Must have IX or SIX for X lock
 END IF
 END IF
 END IF
 
 // Check compatibility with existing locks
 existingLock = GetLock(otherTransactions, node)
 IF NOT Compatible(existingLock, lockType) THEN
 RETURN WAIT // Add to wait queue
 END IF
 
 // Grant lock and update lock table
 GrantLock(transaction, node, lockType)
 RETURN GRANT
END
explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the lock acquisition procedure in MGL. It shows that before acquiring S or X locks at a fine granularity level, the transaction must hold appropriate intent locks on parent nodes. It also checks lock compatibility before granting.

complexity.time: O(h) where h is height of lock hierarchy

title: Lock Compatibility Check Function
description: Function to check if two lock modes are compatible
code:
FUNCTION Compatible(existingLock, requestedLock)
BEGIN
 // Define compatibility matrix
 COMPATIBLE = {
 IS: {IS, S, IX},
 IX: {IS, IX},
 S: {IS, S, IX},
 SIX: {IS},
 X: {NL},
 NL: {IS, IX, S, SIX, X, NL}
 }
 
 IF requestedLock IN COMPATIBLE[existingLock] THEN
 RETURN TRUE
 ELSE
 RETURN FALSE
 END IF
END
explanation: This function implements the lock compatibility matrix. It takes the existing lock and requested lock type, and returns whether they are compatible based on the MGL compatibility rules.

title: Two-Phase Locking with Multiple Granularity
description: Algorithm demonstrating the two-phase locking approach in MGL with growing and shrinking phases
code:
PROCEDURE TwoPhaseMultipleGranularityLock(transaction, operation)
BEGIN
 // Phase 1: Growing Phase - Acquire all needed locks
 FOR EACH node IN DetermineLockHierarchy(operation) DO
 lockType = DetermineLockType(operation, node)
 result = AcquireLock(transaction, node, lockType)
 IF result == WAIT THEN
 // Wait for lock - may cause deadlock
 WaitForLock(transaction, node)
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Execute the operation (all locks acquired)
 ExecuteOperation(operation)
 
 // Phase 2: Shrinking Phase - Release all locks
 // Must release in reverse order (leaf to root)
 locksAcquired = GetAllLocks(transaction)
 ReverseSort(locksAcquired) // Leaf nodes first
 
 FOR EACH lock IN locksAcquired DO
 ReleaseLock(transaction, lock.node, lock.type)
 END FOR
END
explanation: This algorithm demonstrates two-phase locking in MGL. The growing phase acquires locks from root to leaf, then operations are executed, and the shrinking phase releases locks from leaf to root. This ensures serializability.

complexity.time: O(n log n) due to sorting for release

title: Lock Escalation Procedure
description: Algorithm showing how lock escalation works when threshold is reached
code:
PROCEDURE CheckAndEscalate(transaction)
BEGIN
 currentLockCount = CountLocks(transaction)
 threshold = GetEscalationThreshold()
 
 IF currentLockCount > threshold THEN
 // Determine current granularity level
 currentLevel = GetLockGranularity(transaction)
 
 // Cannot escalate beyond file/table level
 IF currentLevel > MIN_GRANULARITY THEN
 // Convert all locks at current level to parent level
 parentLevel = GetParentLevel(currentLevel)
 
 locksAtCurrentLevel = GetLocks(transaction, currentLevel)
 
 // Check if we can acquire parent lock
 FOR EACH lock IN locksAtCurrentLevel DO
 parentNode = GetParent(lock.node)
 requiredLock = DetermineEscalationLock(lock.type)
 
 IF CanAcquire(transaction, parentNode, requiredLock) THEN
 // Release child locks and acquire parent lock
 ReleaseAllChildLocks(transaction, currentLevel)
 AcquireLock(transaction, parentNode, requiredLock)
 RETURN SUCCESS
 ELSE
 // Cannot escalate - wait or continue
 RETURN FAIL
 END IF
 END FOR
 END IF
 END IF
 
 RETURN NO_ESCALATION_NEEDED
END
explanation: This pseudocode shows the lock escalation procedure. When the number of locks exceeds a threshold, the system converts fine-grained locks to coarse-grained locks to reduce overhead.

complexity.time: O(n) where n is number of locks to escalate
